message:
test: add ~23 edge-case tests across alloc and journal crates

ffs-alloc: 9 tests covering GroupStats flags, FsGeometry coordinate
roundtrips, AllocHint defaults, BlockAlloc/InodeAlloc equality, and
bitmap boundary conditions (109 tests, up from 100).

ffs-journal: 14 tests covering JournalSegment resolve, ReplayStats
defaults, Jbd2Writer blocks_needed logic, Jbd2Transaction accessors,
CowWrite equality, and RecoveredCommit equality (156 tests, up from 143).

Minor: rustfmt literal formatting in ffs-btree, linter .clone() removal
in ffs-repair/recovery.rs, fmt in ffs-block.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2oah.4","title":"E2E test: parallel WAL throughput scaling and crash recovery","description":"# E2E test: parallel WAL throughput scaling and crash recovery

## GOAL
Prove that the parallel WAL (Silo/Aether-style per-core buffers with epoch-based ordering) correctly scales write throughput with core count and recovers correctly from crashes at any point in the epoch lifecycle.

## TEST SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Throughput Scaling
1. Run write workload with 1, 2, 4, 8 threads
2. Each thread: 10K transactions, each writing 4 WAL entries
3. Measure: WAL entries/sec, fsync count, epoch count
4. Verify: throughput increases with thread count (near-linear ideal)
5. Verify: fsync count = epoch count (group commit working)
6. Compare against single-WAL baseline

### Scenario 2: Epoch-Boundary Crash Recovery
1. 4 threads writing concurrently
2. Crash at epoch boundary (between epoch E flush and epoch E+1 start)
3. Recover: replay log
4. Verify: all transactions in epochs <= E are committed
5. Verify: no transactions from epoch E+1 visible (incomplete epoch discarded)

### Scenario 3: Mid-Epoch Crash Recovery
1. 4 threads writing concurrently
2. Crash mid-epoch (some per-core buffers partially flushed)
3. Recover: find last complete epoch across all cores
4. Verify: only complete epochs replayed, partial epoch discarded
5. Verify: no partial transactions visible

### Scenario 4: Group Commit Correctness
1. 8 threads, each committing 1 transaction per epoch
2. Verify: all 8 transactions in same epoch share one fsync
3. Verify: await_durability returns only after group fsync complete
4. Verify: no transaction sees committed data before its epoch is durable

### Scenario 5: Epoch Ordering Under High Contention
1. 16 threads, rapid transaction commits (saturate epoch capacity)
2. Verify: epoch ordering is strict (no epoch E+1 entries before epoch E fully flushed)
3. Verify: cross-epoch transactions correctly ordered
4. Verify: recovery produces state equivalent to some serial execution

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Throughput scales >= 3x from 1 to 8 threads
- [ ] fsync count per second is O(epochs), not O(transactions)
- [ ] Epoch-boundary crash: complete epochs recovered, partial discarded
- [ ] Mid-epoch crash: same recovery guarantee
- [ ] No data corruption under any crash scenario
- [ ] All scenarios deterministic and CI-executable
- [ ] Logging: tracing spans for epoch advances, group commits, recovery actions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralReef","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:51.111727981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T01:11:30.609299806Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T01:11:30.609204137Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","e2e","test","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oah.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2oah","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:51.111727981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2oah.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2oah.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:51.111727981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":161,"issue_id":"bd-2oah.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: concurrency_perf
Mapped graveyard sections: 14.10 EBR + 15.1 S3-FIFO + 15.4 Parallel WAL
EV score: 13.333333333333334 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: max_chain_len=64, wal_flush_batch=256, dirty_ratio_cap=0.8
Fallback trigger: single-writer conservative mode with bounded queue

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2oah.5","title":"Add unit tests for parallel WAL buffer and epoch ordering","description":"# Add unit tests for parallel WAL buffer and epoch ordering

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the Parallel WAL epic (bd-2oah). Currently has E2E test (bd-2oah.4) but NO unit tests for per-core WAL buffer, epoch ordering, or group commit logic.

## TEST PLAN

### Per-Core Buffer Tests
1. Each core gets independent WAL buffer
2. Buffer append is lock-free within same core
3. Buffer flush serializes entries correctly
4. Buffer overflow triggers early flush

### Epoch Ordering Tests
5. Entries within same epoch maintain order
6. Cross-core entries ordered by epoch, then core-local sequence
7. Epoch boundary is consistent across all cores
8. Recovery replays entries in epoch order

### Group Commit Tests
9. Multiple transactions in same group commit
10. Group commit waits for all participants
11. Timeout triggers partial group commit
12. Failed transaction excluded from group commit

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
All 12 tests above. Deterministic, no disk I/O (mock device).

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Test failures log WAL buffer state, epoch counters

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 12 tests pass
2. Epoch ordering invariant verified under concurrent load
3. No data loss in group commit edge cases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralReef","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:08.168656588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:55:41.569715186Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:55:41.569652258Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["test","unit","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oah.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2oah","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:08.168656588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2oah.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2oah.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:50.547793012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":158,"issue_id":"bd-2oah.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: max_chain_len=64, wal_flush_batch=256, dirty_ratio_cap=0.8
Fallback trigger: single-writer conservative mode with bounded queue

 {"id":"bd-2oc7","title":"LRC for distributed mode repair (Graveyard Entry 1.4)","description":"# LRC for distributed mode repair (Graveyard Entry 1.4)

## GOAL
Implement Local Reconstruction Codes (LRC) as an alternative to RaptorQ for distributed/multi-node FrankenFS deployments where repair locality matters.

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard 1.4: LRC (Azure LRC, Huang et al. 2012) provides local repair groups within the global erasure code. When a block fails, repair requires only reading from the local group (3-5 blocks) rather than the full erasure group (14+ blocks). This reduces repair I/O by 5-10x for single-block failures.

## DESIGN
- Local groups: partition each block group into 3-4 local repair groups
- Local parity: 1 local parity block per local group
- Global parity: additional global parity blocks for multi-failure
- Repair: single failure reads only local group; multi-failure falls back to global

## APPLICABILITY
- Future feature: only relevant when FrankenFS supports distributed/replicated storage
- Current RaptorQ is superior for single-node (fountain codes handle arbitrary corruption patterns)
- LRC adds value when I/O cost of repair matters (networked storage, slow disks)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] LRC codec implemented (local + global parity)
- [ ] Single-failure repair reads only local group
- [ ] Multi-failure falls back to global reconstruction
- [ ] Benchmark: repair I/O compared to RaptorQ for single vs multi failure","acceptance_criteria":"LRC codec implemented with local + global parity generation. Single-failure repair reads only local group (3-5 blocks, not full 14+ block erasure group). Multi-failure falls back to global reconstruction correctly. Local group boundaries align with block group structure. Evidence records logged for all local vs global repair decisions. Benchmark: repair I/O bytes read for single-failure LRC vs RaptorQ. Unit tests: local parity encode/decode roundtrip. Unit tests: global parity encode/decode roundtrip. Unit tests: single-failure local repair correctness. Unit tests: multi-failure global repair correctness. Property test: any single-block failure repaired by local group alone.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralThrush","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:57.772025290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:42:30.243483274Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T06:42:30.243403394Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distributed","lrc","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oc7","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:33.067392577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":205,"issue_id":"bd-2oc7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: repair_durability
Mapped graveyard sections: 1.1 RaptorQ + 12.13 Change-point + 11.11 PoR
EV score: 25 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: decode_budget_ms=200, io_budget_mb=64, max_repair_attempts=3
Fallback trigger: read-only degrade + evidence ledger + manual repair workflow

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:32Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2phm","title":"Align PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE with current FsOps/OpenFs mount path and ext4+btrfs runtime status","description":"PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md still states ffs-fuse delegates to FrankenFsEngine and claims read-only ext4-only mount status. Update the architecture table/invariants/status text to match current implementation: ffs-fuse delegates to ffs-core::FsOps (OpenFs), both ext4 and btrfs mount paths are present in experimental mode, default RO with optional --rw, and core orchestration naming should reflect OpenFs + FsFlavor dispatch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T01:39:58.201231664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T01:41:15.619302751Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T01:41:15.619282563Z","close_reason":"Completed architecture-doc drift correction for FsOps/OpenFs mount path and runtime status","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","docs"]}
 {"id":"bd-2q7","title":"ext4 semantics: Implement file read (cat) using extent mapping","description":"Goal: read regular file data by mapping logical blocks to physical blocks and reading from BlockDevice.

Deliverables:
- ffs-inode or ffs-core: read_file(inode_no, offset,len) -> bytes.
- Support partial-block reads.
- Handle holes/unwritten extents conservatively (return zeroes or error, per policy).

Acceptance:
- Fixture test reads a known file and matches golden bytes.
- Works through the same path used by FUSE read().","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:26:08.054881047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:41:12.022257501Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:41:12.022235780Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q7","depends_on_id":"bd-10t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:34.205321398Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2q7","depends_on_id":"bd-ye4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:34.090645093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qf","title":"Repair: Extract FrankenSQLite RaptorQ approach and adapt to filesystem blocks","description":"Goal: reuse proven strategy from /dp/frankensqlite for fountain-code-based self-healing, but adapted to ext4/btrfs block groups and filesystem workloads.

Deliverables:
- Summarize the key FrankenSQLite design choices: symbol placement, overhead tuning, decode proofs, ledgering.
- Identify what changes for filesystems: block group boundaries, metadata vs data, read/write patterns.
- Update COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC repair section with an explicit design derived from this extraction.

Acceptance:
- We can explain exactly how a corrupted block is detected and which symbols are used to repair it.
- The design includes tunable parameters and an expected-loss justification (not ad-hoc thresholds).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:24:15.796807569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:40:19.555409837Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:40:19.555343092Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["repair"]}

 {"id":"bd-3nc","title":"ffs-core: Implement ext4 open/validate pipeline on ByteDevice","description":"Goal: open an ext4 image via ffs-block + ffs-ondisk, validate v1 constraints, and produce an Ext4Context used by higher layers.

Deliverables:
- Read ext4 superblock region via ffs-block helper.
- Parse Ext4Superblock and call validate_v1 + geometry validation.
- Construct a BlockDevice using the ext4 block_size.
- Compute group_count and any derived constants needed for later reads.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests: synthetic images + failure cases.
- Fixture test: open a real ext4 image and print key fields (via harness/CLI) without panics.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:59.733624025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:20:35.378477759Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:20:35.378459014Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","ext4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nc","depends_on_id":"bd-1a9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:20:48.250186773Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3nc","depends_on_id":"bd-2w9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:20:48.171049692Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3pm","title":"Track: Self-Healing Durability (ffs-repair + RaptorQ)","description":"Implement scrub + repair workflows using fountain-code repair symbols (RaptorQ) at block-group granularity, inspired by FrankenSQLite.\n\nAcceptance: (1) detect corruption via checksums/invariants, (2) decide repair policy via explicit expected-loss model, (3) generate/store repair symbols with bounded overhead, (4) demonstrate recovery in harness via fault-injection fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:09:03.148300333Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:13:48.907677046Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:13:48.907655636Z","close_reason":"All dependencies closed: scrub pipeline, RaptorQ encode/decode, expected-loss policy, repair format, MVCC engine. Repair + self-healing infrastructure complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pm","depends_on_id":"bd-12x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:24:51.850237578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3pm","depends_on_id":"bd-16f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:24:51.684984729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3pm","depends_on_id":"bd-220","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:25:07.365358636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3pm","depends_on_id":"bd-2qf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:24:51.602555705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3pm","depends_on_id":"bd-98b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:24:51.932081315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3pm","depends_on_id":"bd-b80","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:24:51.767948606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3pm","depends_on_id":"bd-z7n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:25:07.446263615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"bd-3pm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Self-healing must be explainable.

We will not ship "magic" repair heuristics:
- detect corruption via checksums + invariants
- decide redundancy/repair via expected-loss model
- record decode proofs so we can audit why a repair succeeded/failed

This is explicitly patterned after FrankenSQLite, adapted to block groups.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3q4","title":"Harness: Define fixture generation workflow (sparse fixtures + real images)","description":"Goal: make it easy to add fixtures for new parsing/semantics features without ad-hoc manual bytes.

Deliverables:
- Document fixture formats used in conformance/fixtures (sparse JSON today).
- Add a generator approach:
  - option A: produce sparse fixtures from real images by extracting only relevant byte ranges
  - option B: maintain small handcrafted fixtures for unit tests, and use real images for E2E

Acceptance:
- Adding a new fixture is a repeatable recipe (commands + expected outputs) documented in-repo.
- Fixtures are small enough to live in git and stable across platforms.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:26.003872529Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:39:43.151468379Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:39:43.151379713Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["harness"]}
 {"id":"bd-3qq","title":"ext4: Expand inode parsing (timestamps, uid/gid, flags, extent/inline fields)","description":"Goal: parse enough of ext4 inode to support read-only semantics (stat + extent mapping + directory traversal).

Deliverables:
- Parse core inode fields: mode, uid/gid (low/high), size, atime/ctime/mtime/crtime, links, flags, generation, blocks.
- Respect inode_size (128 vs 256) and avoid assuming fixed offsets exist if inode smaller.
- Parse i_block area for extent header and inline extents.

Acceptance:
- Fixture tests compare against debugfs/dumpe2fs for at least a few inodes.
- Parser never panics; InsufficientData is returned for short slices.
- Extent parsing from inode is wired via parse_inode_extent_tree().","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:17:32.338682915Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:19:14.312943367Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:19:14.312924221Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","ondisk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3qq","depends_on_id":"bd-2cn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:06.921918247Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
